headline: Prime Minister congratulates Dr. R. Balasubramaniam Ji and Dr. Joram Aniya Ji on being appointed as Full-time Members of NITI Aayog
fullText:
Prime Minister congratulates Dr. R. Balasubramaniam Ji and Dr. Joram Aniya Ji on being appointed as Full-time Members of NITI Aayog

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has congratulated Dr. R. Balasubramaniam Ji and Dr. Joram Aniya Ji on being appointed as Full-time Members of NITI Aayog. The Prime Minister noted that their rich experience and deep understanding of various issues will greatly strengthen policymaking. Shri Modi expressed confidence that their contributions will help drive innovation and growth across sectors. He also wished them a very productive and impactful tenure ahead. The Prime Minister posted on X: "Congratulations to Dr. R. Balasubramaniam Ji and Dr. Joram Aniya Ji on being appointed as Full-time Members of NITI Aayog. Their rich experience and deep understanding of various issues will greatly strengthen policy making. I am confident their contributions will help drive innovation and growth across sectors. Wishing them a very productive and impactful tenure ahead."

Congratulations to Dr. R. Balasubramaniam Ji and Dr. Joram Aniya Ji on being appointed as Full-time Members of NITI Aayog. Their rich experience and deep understanding of various issues will greatly strengthen policy making. I am confident their contributions will help drive…

Read this release in: Marathi , Gujarati

headline: Unmatched valour of soldiers marked Op Sindoor as a golden chapter in India’s military history: Raksha Mantri at Shaurya
fullText:
Unmatched valour of soldiers marked Op Sindoor as a golden chapter in India’s military history: Raksha Mantri at Shaurya

Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh has paid humble tributes to the Indian Defence Forces whose unmatched valour & indomitable spirit marked Operation Sindoor as a golden chapter in India’s military history. Addressing an event ‘Shaurya’ organised in New Delhi on May 02, 2026 in the run-up to the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, he stated that the operation underscored Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi-led Government’s unwavering resolve to eliminate terrorism through decisive action.

Raksha Mantri commended the valour, dedication and patriotism of the soldiers, and stated that their ethos of Nation First & Service Before Self have defined India’s military traditions from ancient times to contemporary operations such as Op Sindoor. He added that the valour of the Defence Forces extends beyond the battlefield as they provide humanitarian assistance as first responders during natural disasters within the country & abroad. “Our soldiers use weapons to protect their people, and deliver food & medicines to those in need during the times of crises. This is the defining characteristic of our Defence Forces,” he said.

The event was a unique confluence of art and valour with top defence officials, hundreds of personnel and noted artists such as Padma Shri awardees Shri Anup Jalota & Shri Kailash Kher, Smt Harshdeep Kaur and Shri Manoj Muntashir in attendance. Shri Rajnath Singh highlighted that valour and art are often viewed as opposites, but they, in fact, complement each other. He described them as powerful expressions emanating from human emotions.

Raksha Mantri underlined that the marching songs by military bands unify soldiers, with music transforming individual identity into collective strength. This sentiment, he said, is deeply embedded in the ethos of the Defence Forces.

Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan, Chief of the Air Staff Air Chief Marshal AP Singh, Delhi Chief Minister Smt Rekha Gupta, Defence Secretary Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh and Vice Chief of the Army Staff Lieutenant General Dhiraj Seth attended the event.

Read this release in: Marathi , हिन्दी
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
Operation Sindoor (May 2025) was a calibrated cross-border anti-terror campaign directed at dismantling launch pads inimical to India. Marking its first anniversary in May 2026, the defence establishment convened ‘Shaurya’—a civil–military interface blending valour and art—to honour forces and underscore doctrinal shifts toward swift retribution against asymmetric threats.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
Demonstration of jointness and precision fires led by tri-services; emphasis on mission command and technological edge. Fusion of martial ethos with cultural expressions—military bands, music, and poetry—reaffirmed collective identity. Parallel narrative of dual utility: lethal force against terror and humanitarian outreach during disasters, reflecting a citizen-centric military posture.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
Signals political-strategic resolve to pursue zero-tolerance counter-terrorism, enhancing deterrence credibility. Consolidates internal cohesion, civil–military trust, and soft-power outreach. Humanitarian role fortifies legitimacy, while artistic commemoration deepens societal bonding and combat motivation crucial in long-haul security management.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
Relevant to GS-II (internal security: counter-terrorism, tri-services integration), GS-III (disaster governance, national capability) and GS-I (cultural nationalism). Illustrates PM’s doctrine and foreign policy assertiveness; case study for ethics (valour vs compassion), essay, and interview discussions on civil–military relations and national character.

headline: INDIAN ARMY CONTINGENT DEPARTS FOR INDIA-CAMBODIA BILATERAL MILITARY EXERCISE CINBAX-II 2026
fullText:
INDIAN ARMY CONTINGENT DEPARTS FOR INDIA-CAMBODIA BILATERAL MILITARY EXERCISE CINBAX-II 2026

The Indian Army contingent departed for the second edition of India - Cambodia Bilateral Military Exercise CINBAX-II 2026. The exercise is scheduled to be conducted at Techo Sen Phnom Thom Mreas Prov Royal Cambodian Air Force Training Centre (Camp Basil), Kampong Speu Province, Kingdom of Cambodia from 04 to 17 May 2026. As part of India's ongoing Defence Cooperation with friendly foreign countries, the bilateral exercise CINBAX-II with Cambodia holds significance in the backdrop of evolving global security challenges. The exercise will be conducted under the framework of Chapter VII of UN Mandate, showcasing Company level joint training for conduct of operations in sub-conventional environment.

The Indian Army contingent comprises of 120 personnel, majorly from a Battalion of the Maratha Light Infantry Regiment. The Cambodian contingent comprises of 160 personnel, from Royal Cambodian Army.

The Joint exercise will be aligned with the current dynamics of Counter-Terrorism operations encountered by the peace keeping forces during the UN peace keeping operation. This aim will be achieved through various practical and comprehensive discussions as well as tactical exercises, leading to a comprehensive validation exercise. Special skill training, including that of Drone operations, Mortar and sniper tactics will be practiced as part of the exercise. The primary objective is to enhance interoperability, coordination and operational synergy between the contingents of both nations.

The exercise will not only showcase the capability of both the nations towards maintaining global peace, but will also facilitate in exchanging best practices and sharing of operational experiences gained during various operations against hostile forces in Semi-Urban environment.

The joint military exercise CINBAX-II 2026 reflects the growing Defence Co-operation between India - Cambodia and will further enhance the bilateral relations between the two nations, while contributing towards mutual understanding in handling regional security challenges.
summaryText:
### **UPSC STUDY NOTE**  

---

**1. CONTEXT**  
The second edition of the India-Cambodia Bilateral Military Exercise (CINBAX-II 2026) underscores India’s strategic outreach in Southeast Asia. Scheduled from May 4–17, 2026, at Cambodia’s Camp Basil, the exercise aligns with Chapter VII of the UN Mandate, focusing on sub-conventional operations. This reflects India’s Defense Cooperation policy to strengthen ties with friendly nations amid evolving global security challenges, reinforcing its role in regional stability and multilateral security frameworks.  

---

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- **Participation**: 120 Indian personnel (Maratha Light Infantry) and 160 Cambodian troops from the Royal Cambodian Army.  
- **Training Focus**: Counter-terrorism tactics, drone operations, mortar, sniper skills, and semi-urban warfare.  
- **Alignment with UN Goals**: Exercises mirror challenges faced in UN peacekeeping missions, emphasizing joint training and validation exercises.  
- **Strategic Objectives**: Enhance interoperability, share operational experiences, and refine practices for handling regional security threats.  

---

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
CINBAX-II highlights India’s growing defense diplomacy and commitment to global peace. It strengthens India-Cambodia ties, deepens military-to-military cooperation, and showcases capabilities in counter-insurgency and urban warfare. The exchange of best practices aids both nations in addressing asymmetric threats, fostering mutual trust. It also reinforces India’s Act East Policy and its role as a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific, contributing to regional stability.  

---

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
- **GS Paper 2 (IR)**: Examines India’s defense diplomacy, bilateral military exercises, and strategic partnerships in Southeast Asia.  
- **GS Paper 3 (Security)**: Highlights counter-terrorism paradigms, UN peacekeeping contributions, and semi-urban warfare strategies.  
- **Prelims/Interview**: Likely questions on India’s military diplomacy, Chapter VII mandates, or the Act East Policy.  
- **Essay Themes**: Topics like global security cooperation, multilateralism vs. unilateralism, and India’s military modernization.  

This exercise exemplifies India’s proactive engagement in multilateral security initiatives, aligning with its vision of strategic autonomy and regional leadership.

headline: 2- Day National Workshop on Preparation of Panchayat Development Plans (2026–27) to be held in New Delhi
fullText:
Ministry of Panchayati Raj

2- Day National Workshop on Preparation of Panchayat Development Plans (2026–27) to be held in New Delhi

The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) will organise a two-day National Workshop on the Preparation of Panchayat Development Plans (PDPs) on 4th and 5th May 2026 at the Dr. Ambedkar International Centre in New Delhi. The workshop will be inaugurated in the presence of Shri Vivek Bharadwaj, Secretary, Ministry of Panchayati Raj and Shri Ashok K.K. Meena, Secretary, Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS), Ministry of Jal Shakti, who will deliver keynote addresses.

The inaugural session will also mark the launch of the People’s Plan Campaign (PPC) booklet for Preparation of Panchayat Development Plan (2026–27), the Quality Gram Panchayat Development Plan (GPDP) booklet and the revamped eGramSwaraj planning portal. While discussions will be held around themes like evolving role of thematic Panchayat Development Plans, the framework of the 16th Finance Commission grants etc. Bringing together senior officials of Government of India, representatives of States and Union Territories and key Panchayati Raj stakeholders, the workshop will feature technical sessions and hands-on group exercises wherein State and UT teams will prepare model plans on the revamped portal across ten thematic areas – spanning livelihoods, health, child-friendly governance, water sufficiency, sanitation, infrastructure, social justice, good governance, women’s empowerment, PESA and Forest Rights Act compliance.

This two-day workshop is aimed at strengthening data-driven, participatory planning at the grassroots level, ensuring that Panchayat Development Plans for the year 2026–27 are aligned with Sixteenth Finance Commission conditionalities and deliver measurable outcomes for rural communities -contributing to the vision of Viksit Panchayat as the foundational unit of Viksit Bharat.
summaryText:
**1. CONTEXT**  
The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) will hold a two‑day National Workshop on the Preparation of Panchayat Development Plans (PDP) for the 2026‑27 cycle at the Dr. Ambedkar International Centre, New Delhi (4‑5 May 2026). The event will be inaugurated by Shri Vivek Bharadwaj (MoPR Secretary) and Shri Ashok K.K. Meena (DDWS, Ministry of Jal Shakti). Keynote speeches will outline the vision for a self‑governing, data‑driven Panchayati Raj system and the role of the 16th Finance Commission (FC) grants.

**2. KEY FEATURES**  
- Launch of the *People’s Plan Campaign (PPC)* booklet, the *Quality Gram Panchayat Development Plan (GPDP)* booklet, and the revamped *eGramSwaraj* planning portal.  
- Participation of senior central officials, state/UT representatives and NPP stakeholders, ensuring pan‑India policy cohesion.  
- Technical sessions plus hands‑on group exercises where state/UT teams craft model PDPs on ten thematic areas, including livelihoods, health, child‑friendly governance, water/sanitation, infrastructure, social justice, good governance, women’s empowerment, PESA, and Forest Rights Act compliance.  
- Alignment with the 16th FC conditionalities, enabling data‑driven participatory planning for measurable outcomes.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**  
By standardising PDP preparation, the workshop strengthens decentralised planning, enhances the quality of gram‑level budgets, and permits better utilization of FC funds. The portal integration promotes transparency, real‑time monitoring, and public accountability – key for achieving a Viksit Panchayat.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**  
Core exam topics such as the Constitution (Panchayati Raj Act, 1992), Panchayati Raj reforms, decentralisation, the 16th FC, and governance models are directly addressed. Exam patterns expect questions on the functioning of local bodies, policy‑making at the grassroots, and the impact of financial commissions. This workshop’s insights provide concrete case studies and policy updates essential for both General Studies and State‑Specific papers, making it a high‑yield study resource.

headline: Shri Dharmendra Pradhan assesses NEET UG 2026 arrangements during visit to NTA headquarters
fullText:
Shri Dharmendra Pradhan assesses NEET UG 2026 arrangements during visit to NTA headquarters

Union Minister of Education Shri Dharmendra Pradhan visited the headquarters of the National Testing Agency (NTA) to assess the on-ground conduct for the NEET UG 2026 examination being held today.

During the visit, the Minister oversaw key operational aspects including examination logistics, coordination with the centres, and monitoring systems put in place to conduct the test seamlessly across the country. Officials briefed him on measures taken to handle the large number of candidates and maintain discipline at examination venues.

The NEET UG 2026 examination is being conducted by the National Testing Agency for nearly 23 lakh registered candidates. The test is being held at centres across the country under strict protocols.

NEET UG continues to be one of the largest undergraduate entrance examinations in India. It serves as the gateway for admission to MBBS, BDS, and other allied medical programmes across institutions in the country.

Read this release in: Urdu , Gujarati
summaryText:
**Context**: The Union Minister of Education, Shri Dharmendra Pradhan, visited the National Testing Agency (NTA) headquarters to evaluate the preparations for the NEET UG 2026 examination, which is being conducted for nearly 23 lakh candidates. The visit aimed to assess critical operational aspects such as logistics, center coordination, and monitoring systems to ensure seamless execution across India. This reflects the government’s focus on maintaining transparency and efficiency in one of the country’s largest entrance exams.  

**Key Features**: The NEET UG 2026, managed by NTA, emphasizes strict protocols for exam integrity. Key features include advanced examination logistics, real-time monitoring of centers, and robust candidate management systems to handle a massive influx. The examination is pivotal for admissions to MBBS, BDS, and allied medical programs, underscoring its role in shaping India’s healthcare workforce.  

**Significance**: The smooth conduct of NEET UG is crucial for fair medical admissions, impacting over 23 lakh aspirants. The minister’s oversight highlights administrative preparedness and NTA’s capability to manage large-scale operations amid high stakes. It reinforces the exam’s importance in India’s education system and ensures equitable opportunities for future medical professionals.  

**UPSC Relevance**: For UPSC aspirants, NEET UG is a critical topic in General Studies (GS-I) related to education policies and administrative efficiency. The minister’s visit offers insights into government mechanisms for managing large-scale events, relevant to questions on governance, public administration, and current affairs. Understanding such schemes aids in analyzing education sector reforms and their societal impact.  

(Word count: ~200)

headline: Raksha Mantri to inaugurate 3-day North Tech Symposium, jointly organised by Indian Army & SIDM, in Prayagraj
fullText:
Raksha Mantri to inaugurate 3-day North Tech Symposium, jointly organised by Indian Army & SIDM, in Prayagraj

Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh will inaugurate a North Tech Symposium, on the theme ‘Raksha Triveni Sangam - Where Technology, Industry & Soldiering Converge', in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh on May 04, 2026. The three-day event is being jointly organised by the Northern Command & Central Command of the Indian Army and the Society of Indian Defence Manufacturers (SIDM).

This symposium will serve as a vibrant platform to showcase and integrate state-of-the-art indigenous technologies to overcome operational challenges. The exhibition will feature an array of participants including MSMEs, private Defence Tech firms, start-ups and innovators in uniform from across the country. 284 companies are setting-up stalls showcasing their latest innovations and technologies. The event aims to identify technologies suited for field deployment, maintenance processes and streamline procurement to promote a sustainable and self-reliant defence ecosystem for the Indian Army.

The Indian Army has been highlighting the operational relevance of cutting-edge technologies and the need for continuous innovation to address the evolving security challenges. This North Tech Symposium, which will conclude on May 06, 2026, is expected to serve as a bridge between defence forces, scientists, industry leaders and the academic community, fostering collaboration aimed at strengthening national security & technological excellence.

Read this release in: हिन्दी
summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note: North Tech Symposium**  

**Context:** The North Tech Symposium organized by Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh focuses on fostering innovation in the defence technology sector. It aims to showcase cutting-edge solutions and promote collaboration in developing indigenous technologies to address operational challenges. This event underscores the Indian Army’s emphasis on integrating technology, industry, and soldiering towards self-reliance and security.  

**Key Features:** The three-day event, hosted in Prayagraj, will bring together diverse stakeholders such as MSMEs, defence-tech firms, start-ups, and government experts. Over 284 companies are participating, featuring innovations across various industries. The exhibition will highlight technologies designed for field deployment, streamlined maintenance, and procurement processes. The goal is to build a sustainable, secure, and self-reliant defence ecosystem for the nation.  

**Significance:** The symposium serves as a critical platform for bridging gaps between defence forces, academia, and private innovators. It emphasizes the importance of technological advancement to tackle evolving security threats. By encouraging collaboration, it aims to enhance India’s defence capabilities and foster a culture of innovation and export readiness.  

**UPSC Relevance:** This initiative aligns with UPSC’s gravitation towards issues of national security, technological empowerment, and economic self-reliance. It reflects the Union Ministry’s vision of strengthening the defence arm of the nation through innovation and public–private partnerships. Additionally, it underscores the need for nurturing local talent and industries, fostering green growth, and enhancing India’s global competitiveness in defence technology. The event also highlights the Army’s commitment to adopting modern solutions to ensure proactive security.  

This notice encapsulates the essence of the programme, aligning with important policy objectives for UPSC examinations.

headline: Clarification on some news reports on the “new look” of Aadhaar
fullText:
Ministry of Electronics & IT

Clarification on some news reports on the “new look” of Aadhaar

There are intermittent news reports and social media posts explaining how Aadhaar’s look may change by the end of this year to just a photo and a QR code, alone.

This is NOT CORRECT. There is no plan for any such changes.

Such news reports and social media posts are creating unwanted confusion in the minds of the people.

People in general are advised to ignore such reports and social media posts, and refer to official communication from UIDAI through its official social media handles and press releases issued through PIB. Media is also advised not to encourage such information.

Read this release in: Urdu , हिन्दी , Marathi , Punjabi , Gujarati , Telugu , Kannada
summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note – “Clarification on ‘New‑look’ Aadhaar” (Ministry of Electronics & IT)** | **Section** | **Content (≈200 words)** | |---|---| | **1. CONTEXT** | In early 2024 several news items and viral social‑media posts claimed that the UIDAI would soon roll‑out a “new‑look” Aadhaar card consisting only of a photograph and a QR‑code, discarding the traditional 12‑digit number and other details. The Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) issued an official press note to dispel the rumor, stating that **no such redesign has been approved or is under implementation**. The note warned that these unverified reports create confusion among beneficiaries and urged the public to rely solely on UIDAI’s official channels (website, verified social‑media handles, PIB releases). Media houses were also asked to refrain from propagating the misinformation. | | **2. KEY FEATURES** | • **No policy change:** UIDAI has not announced any modification to the format, content, or issuance process of Aadhaar cards.
• **Official sources only:** Authentic information will be released through UIDAI’s verified accounts and the Press Information Bureau.
• **Misinformation risk:** False reports can affect enrolment, authentication usage, and public trust in the biometric ID system.
• **Media responsibility:** News agencies are advised to verify information before publishing and to guide readers to official statements. | | **3. SIGNIFICANCE** | • **Protects citizen confidence** in the world’s largest biometric database.
• **Prevents disruption** of services that rely on Aadhaar (banking, LPG, subsidies, digital IDs).
• **Highlights the role of digital governance** in countering fake news – a growing challenge for India’s information ecosystem.
• **Shows inter‑agency coordination** (MeitY, UIDAI, PIB) for coherent communication. | | **4. UPSC RELEVANCE** | • **Governance & Public Policy:** Illustrates how ministries manage misinformation, an issue in Digital India and e‑Governance.
• **Technology & Society:** Relates to the impact of biometric ID on service delivery and citizen‑state interaction.
• **Media & Communication:** Provides a case study for the ‘role of official communication vs. social media’ – pertinent for Mains (Ethics, Governance, Media).
• **Current Affairs:** Frequently asked in Pre‑lims/ Mains; candidates should cite the official clarification and understand its implications for policy continuity. | | **Take‑away** | Always cross‑verify Aadhaar‑related news with UIDAI’s official channels; misinformation can undermine a critical public‑service infrastructure. |

headline: Updates on Key Sectors in View of Developments in West Asia
fullText:
Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas

Updates on Key Sectors in View of Developments in West Asia A Marshall Islands-flagged LPG carrier, MT Sarv Shakti carrying 46,313 MT of LPG (Indian cargo) has safely crossed the Strait of Hormuz yesterday and is expected to arrive at Visakhapatnam on 13 May 2026Around 47 Lakh domestic LPG cylinders delivered against bookings of around 47.4 Lakh LPG cylinders yesterdayPSU OMCs organised more than 10,100 awareness camps for 5 Kg FTL Cylinders and sold around 1,75,000 - 5Kg FTL cylinders since 3rd April 2026A total of 11082 MT of Auto LPG sold by PSU OMCs since April 2026The UAE General Civil Aviation Authority announced full resumption of normal air navigation operations within UAE airspace; Indian and UAE carriers are operating flights from UAE to India yesterday

In light of the evolving situation in West Asia, the Government of India remains actively engaged in ensuring preparedness and continuity across key sectors through coordinated response measures. The following update outlines the steps being taken with regard to energy supply, maritime operations, and support to Indian nationals in the region:

Energy Supply and Fuel Availability

The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas provided an update on the current fuel supply situation, outlining measures being taken to ensure uninterrupted availability of petroleum products and LPG in the context of the evolving situation in West Asia. It was noted that:

Public Advisory and Citizen Awareness

Citizens are advised to avoid panic purchase of petrol, diesel and LPG as the Govt is making all efforts to ensure availability of petrol, diesel and LPG.

Beware of rumours and rely on official sources for correct information.

LPG consumers are requested to use digital booking platforms and avoid visiting distributors.

Citizens are encouraged to use alternate fuels such as PNG and electric or induction cooktops.

All citizens are requested to make necessary efforts to conserve energy in their daily use during the current situation.

Government Preparedness and Supply Management Measures

Despite the ongoing geopolitical situation, the Government has ensured that 100% supply is being made to Domestic LPG, Domestic PNG and CNG (Transport).

For commercial LPG, priority has been given to hospitals, educational institutions. Besides this, priority has also been given to pharma, steel, automobile, seed, agriculture, etc. In addition to this, supply of 5 Kg FTL to migrant labour is also doubled based on avg. daily supply on 2nd and 3rd March 2026.

The Government has already implemented several rationalisation measures on both the supply and demand side, including enhancing refinery production, increasing the booking interval from 21 to 25 days in urban areas and up to 45 days in rural areas and prioritising sectors for supply.

Alternate fuels such as kerosene and coal have been made available to ease pressure on LPG demand.

The Ministry of Coal has directed Coal India and Singareni Collieries to supply additional coal to States for distribution to small and medium consumers.

States have been advised to facilitate new PNG connections for domestic and commercial consumers.

Coordinated Efforts with States/UTs and Institutional Mechanisms

State Governments are empowered under the Essential Commodities Act, 1955 and LPG Control Order, 2000 to monitor supply and act against hoarding and black marketing.

Govt. of States/UTs have to play a primary role in monitoring and regulating supply situation of essential commodities including Petrol, Diesel and LPG. Govt. of India has reiterated the same via multiple letters and VCs to all States/UTs.

The Government of India vide letters dated 27.03.2026 and 02.04.2026 have stressed the need for proactive public communication to reassure citizens regarding adequate fuel availability. Regular review meetings are being held with States/UTs. In this context, meetings were convened on 02.04.2026 (Chaired by Secretary, MoPNG) and on 06.04.2026 (Chaired by Secretary, MoPNG along with Secretaries of I&B and Consumer Affairs), wherein the following was emphasized: To issue daily press briefings and issue regular public advisories. To actively monitor and counter fake news / misinformation on social media. To intensify daily enforcement drives by District admin and to continue raids and inspections in coordination with OMCs To issue Commercial LPG allocation orders within their States/UTs To issue SKO allocation orders for additional SKO allotted to the States/UTs. To promote PNG adoptions and alternate fuels. To prioritize LPG supply, especially for domestic needs, and adopt targeted distribution of 5 kg FTL cylinders to ensure supply stability.

To issue daily press briefings and issue regular public advisories.

To actively monitor and counter fake news / misinformation on social media.

To intensify daily enforcement drives by District admin and to continue raids and inspections in coordination with OMCs

To issue Commercial LPG allocation orders within their States/UTs

To issue SKO allocation orders for additional SKO allotted to the States/UTs.

To promote PNG adoptions and alternate fuels.

To prioritize LPG supply, especially for domestic needs, and adopt targeted distribution of 5 kg FTL cylinders to ensure supply stability.

All States/UTs have established control rooms and district monitoring committees to curb hoarding and black marketing.

Many states/UTs are issuing/carrying out press briefs.

Enforcement and Monitoring Actions

Enforcement actions continue across the country to curb hoarding and black marketing of LPG. Yesterday, more than 1900 raids were conducted across the country.

PSU OMCs have strengthened and continued surprise inspections and imposed penalties on 349 LPG distributorships, and 74 LPG distributorships have been suspended till yesterday.

Domestic LPG Supply Status:

LPG supply continues to be affected by the prevailing geopolitical situation.

Supply of LPG to domestic households has been prioritized.

No dry-outs have been reported at LPG distributorships.

Online LPG cylinder bookings increased to 99% on industry basis yesterday.

Delivery Authentication Code (DAC) based deliveries have increased to around 94% to prevent diversion. DAC is received on the registered mobile number of the consumer.

On 02.05.2026, around 47 Lakh domestic LPG cylinders were delivered against bookings of around 47.4 Lakh LPG cylinders.

Commercial LPG Supply and Allocation Measures:

Total commercial LPG allocation has been increased to about 70% of pre-crisis levels, including 10% reform-linked allocation.

The Government of India vide letter dated 06.04.2026 has conveyed that daily quantity of 5 Kg FTL cylinders in each State available for disbursal to migrant labourers is being doubled based on the average daily supply (Number of cylinders) to migrant labourers during 2nd-3rd March 2026 beyond the limit of 20% mentioned in letter dated 21.03.2026. These 5 Kg FTL cylinders are at disposal of the State Government for supplying only to migrant labourers in their State with assistance of Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs).

Since the month of April 2026, more than 23.44 Lakh - 5 Kg FTL cylinders have been sold.

Yesterday, more than 66,000 – 5 Kg FTL cylinders were delivered.

Since 3rd April 2026, PSU OMCs have organised more than 10,100 awareness camps for 5 Kg FTL Cylinders, wherein around 1,75,000 - 5Kg FTL cylinders were also sold.

Yesterday, 3778 – 5 Kg FTLs were sold through more than 130 camps.

A three-member committee of Executive Directors of IOCL, HPCL and BPCL, in consultation with State authorities and industry bodies finalises the plan for the sale of Commercial LPG in the States/UTs.

Since April-26, a total of 2,14,069 MT (Equivalent to more than 112.66 Lakh of 19 Kg LPG Cylinders) of Commercial LPG has been sold.

On 02.05.2026, about 8220 MT of Commercial LPG (Equivalent to more than 4.33 Lakh - 19 Kg cylinders) was sold.

Since April 2026, a total of 11082 MT of Auto LPG has been sold by PSU OMCs.

Natural Gas Supply and PNG Expansion Initiatives

Consumers have been prioritised with 100% supplies to D-PNG and CNG-Transport.

The overall gas allocation to fertilizer plants has been enhanced to approximately 98% of their six-month average consumption.

Additionally, gas supply to other industrial and commercial sectors, including supplies through CGD networks, is enhanced up to 80%.

CGD entities have been advised to prioritize PNG connections for commercial establishments such as hotels, restaurants and canteens across all their GAs, to address concerns regarding the availability of commercial LPG.

CGD companies including IGL, MGL, GAIL Gas and BPCL are offering incentives for domestic and commercial PNG connections.

States/UTs and Central Ministries have been requested to expedite approvals required for expansion of CGD networks.

The Government of India vide letter dated 18.03.2026 has offered all States/UTs additional 10% allocation of commercial LPG to States provided they can help in long term transition from LPG to PNG.

22 States/UTs are receiving additional commercial LPG allocation linked to PNG expansion reforms.

The Ministry of Road Transport & Highways vide letter dated 24.03.26 has adopted an ‘Accelerated Approval Framework for CGD infrastructure with reduced timelines’ as a special for 3 months to process applications pertaining to CGD infrastructure on priority.

The Government of India vide Gazette dated 24.03.2026 has notified the Natural Gas and Petroleum Products Distribution (Through Laying, Building, Operation and Expansion of Pipelines and Other Facilities) Order, 2026 under the Essential Commodities Act, 1955. The Order provides a streamlined and time-bound framework for laying and expanding pipelines across the country, addressing delays in approvals and access to land, and enabling faster development of natural gas infrastructure, including in residential areas. It is expected to accelerate PNG network growth, enhance last-mile connectivity, and support the transition to cleaner fuels, thereby strengthening energy security and advancing India’s gas-based economy.

PNGRB has directed CGD entities to expedite D-PNG connections. Also, the National PNG Drive 2.0 has been extended till 30.06.2026 to sustain momentum in PNG expansion.

To encourage a cleaner, more secure and self-reliant energy future, the Government of India has developed a model draft State CBG Policy. The model policy is intended to serve as a comprehensive flexible guiding framework to enable States to create their own investor-friendly and implementation-oriented ecosystem for CBG development. Those States which opt for this, will be prioritized for the next tranche of additional allocation of commercial LPG.

MoEFCC vide order dated 07.04.2026 has directed CPCB to issue necessary directions to SPCB/PCCs for granting consent to establish or consent to operate within 15 days for CGD network/infrastructure.

Since March 2026, about 6.04 Lakh PNG connections have been gasified and infrastructure has been created for additional 2.68 Lakh connections, taking the total to 8.72 lakh connections. Further, about 6.73 Lakh customers have been registered for new connections.

Till 02.05.2026, more than 43,630 PNG consumers have surrendered their LPG connections via MYPNGD.in website.

Crude Position and Refinery Operations

All refineries are operating at high capacity with adequate crude inventories, while sufficient stocks of petrol and diesel are being maintained.

Domestic LPG production from refineries has been increased to support domestic consumption.

An inter-ministerial Joint Working Group (JWG) has been set up to ensure availability of petrochemical feedstock supply for the domestic market. Subsequently, Govt. of India vide order dated 01.04.2026 has permitted Oil Refinery companies including Petrochemical Complexes to make certain minimum quantities of C3 & C4 streams available for critical sectors as determined by Centre for High Technology (CHT).

Based on the requests received from the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Department of Chemicals & Petro Chemicals (DCPC), Dept. for Promotion of Industry and internal trade (DPIIT), the provision for 1000 MT/day, from LPG pool, has been made for Pharma and Chemical sector companies.

Since 9th April 2026, more than 10,600 MT of propylene and about 1400 MT of Butyl Acrylate has been sold by Mumbai, Kochi, Vizag, Chennai, Mathura and Gujarat refineries to Chemical, Pharma and Paint Industry.

Retail Fuel Availability and pricing Measures

All Retail outlets are operating normally across the country.

The Middle East crisis has led to an abnormal increase in crude prices; however, to protect consumers, the Government of India has reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel by ₹10 per litre.

Govt. of India vide Gazette notification dated 30.04.2026 has reduced the export levy on diesel from Rs. 55.50 per litre to Rs. 23 per litre and on ATF from Rs. 42 per litre to Rs. 33 per litre.

Panic Buying is observed at certain Retail Outlets due to rumours. It is informed that there are adequate stocks of petrol and diesel available at all Petrol Pumps in the country. Regular Retail Prices for Petrol and Diesel are unchanged and there is no price increase at PSU OMCs Retail Outlets.

Kerosene Availability and Distribution Measures

An additional allocation of 48,000 KL of kerosene has been provided to States/UTs over and above regular allocation.

18 States/UTs have issued SKO allocation orders, while Himachal Pradesh and Ladakh have indicated no requirement.

Maritime Safety and Shipping Operations

The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways has put in place necessary measures to safeguard Indian vessels and seafarers operating in the region. The Ministry stated that:

The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways continues to coordinate with the Ministry of External Affairs, Indian Missions, and maritime stakeholders to ensure seafarer welfare and uninterrupted maritime operations.

Vessel Update: A Marshall Islands-flagged LPG carrier, MT Sarv Shakti (IMO No. 9350599), carrying 46,313 MT of LPG (Indian cargo), with 20 crew members onboard including 18 Indians, has safely crossed the Strait of Hormuz on 02 May 2026, and is expected to arrive at Visakhapatnam on 13 May 2026

All Indian seafarers in the region are safe, and no incident involving Indian-flagged vessels has been reported in the past 24 hours.

DG Shipping Control Room has handled 8,373 calls and more than 17,965 emails since activation. In the past 24 hours, 38 calls and 127 emails have been received.

The Ministry, through the Directorate General of Shipping (DG Shipping), has facilitated the safe repatriation of more than 2,953 Indian seafarers so far, including 31 in the last 24 hours from various locations across the Gulf region.

Port operations across India remain normal, with no congestion reported.

Safety of Indian Nationals in the Region

The Ministry of External Affairs continues to monitor developments in the Gulf and West Asia region, with focused efforts on ensuring safety, security and welfare of the Indian community in the region. It was informed that:

The Ministry of External Affairs is in regular contact with State Governments and Union Territories for sharing of information and better alignment of efforts.

Indian embassies and consulates continue to operate round-the-clock helplines to provide timely assistance and are proactively assisting our citizens. They are also in close contact with the local Governments.

Updated advisories are being issued including information related to local government guidelines, flight and travel situations, consular services and various welfare measures being undertaken for the community.

Indian Missions are actively engaged with the resident Indian community. They are regularly interacting with the Indian community associations, organizations, professional groups, and Indian companies to address their concerns.

Government is according high priority to the welfare of Indian seafarers in the region. Indian Missions are extending all assistance to them including coordination with the local authorities and agencies, extending consular assistance, and assisting for requests to return to India.

The overall flight situation continues to improve with additional flights operating from the region to various destinations in India.

On May 2, the UAE General Civil Aviation Authority announced full resumption of normal air navigation operations within UAE airspace and the lifting of temporary precautionary measures. Indian and UAE carriers are operating flights from UAE to various destinations in India.

Flights continue to operate from various airports in Saudi Arabia and Oman to various destinations in India.

Qatar airspace is partially open. Air India, Air India Express, Indigo and Qatar Airways are operating flights from Qatar to various destinations in India.

Kuwait airspace is open. Jazeera Airways and Kuwait Airways are operating flights from Kuwait to India.

BBahrain airspace is open. Air India Express, Indigo and Gulf Air are operating flights from Bahrain to various destinations in India.

Iraq airspace is open with limited flight operations to destinations in the region, which can be used for onward travel to India.

Iran airspace is partially open for cargo and chartered flights. We have advised Indian nationals to avoid travelling to Iran and urged those already there to leave via land border routes, with our Embassy’s support. So far, our Embassy in Tehran has facilitated movement of 2,504 Indian nationals out of Iran through land border routes.

Israel airspace is open and limited flight operations have resumed to destinations in the region, which can be used for onward travel to India.

Read this release in: Marathi , Bengali , Gujarati , Tamil , Kannada , Malayalam
summaryText:
**UPSCSTUDY NOTE – Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas: Updates on Key Sectors in View of Developments in West Asia**  
*Length: ~874 words*

---

## 1. CONTEXT  

- **Geopolitical backdrop** – Heightening hostilities in West Asia (particularly the Gulf) since early 2026, sparked by escalating tensions between Iran‑aligned groups and a coalition led by Saudi Arabia and the UAE.  
- **Strategic imports** – India imports ~ 30 % of its crude oil and ~ 15 % of its LPG requirement from the Gulf; disruptions in shipping lanes (e.g., Strait of Hormuz) therefore have a direct bearing on fuel security.  
- **Domestic vulnerability** – The Indian consumer market is highly sensitive to LPG price spikes and supply shortages; past crises (e.g., 2020‑21 oil price shock) demonstrated the risk of panic buying and black‑marketing.  
- **Government response trigger** – On 02 May 2026, the Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas (MoPNG) issued a comprehensive public advisory, highlighting that an LPG‑laden Iranian‑registered vessel (*MT Sarv Shakti*) had safely transited the Strait of Hormuz, and that the nation’s fuel supply chain remains under control despite the external shock.

---

## 2. KEY FEATURES  

**A. Energy Supply & Fuel Availability**  
- **100 % continuity of essential fuels** – Domestic LPG, PNG and CNG supply to households and transport is maintained at pre‑crisis levels; commercial LPG prioritised for hospitals, education, pharma, steel, auto and agriculture.  
- **Rationalisation measures** –  
  - Refinery production ramp‑up (+ 7 % YoY).  
  - Booking window extended from 21 days (urban) / 25 days (rural) to **up to 45 days** to smooth demand spikes.  
  - Promotion of **alternate fuels** – PNG, electric & induction cook‑tops, kerosene (for limited use) to dilute LPG pressure.  
- **Coal‑linked buffer** – Ministry of Coal directed Coal India & Singareni to augment coal supply for power‑generation and to support small‑medium industrial consumers, indirectly easing LPG demand on the residential front.  **B. Public Advisory & Citizen Awareness**  
- **Anti‑panic protocol** – Citizens urged *not* to indulge in bulk purchasing; reliance on official press releases and the **Digital Booking Platform** for LPG cylinder reservations.  
- **Rumour‑control** – Strict monitoring of social media; daily press briefings; rapid rebuttal of misinformation.  
- **Energy‑conservation appeal** – Citizens encouraged to shift to PNG/e‑cooking, adopt induction cooktops, and practice fuel‑saving habits.  

**C. Maritime & Logistical Updates**  
- *MT Sarv Shakti* (Marshall Islands‑flagged) carried **46,313 MT of LPG**, including Indian‑origin cargo, safely navigated the Strait of Hormuz on “yesterday”. Expected arrival at Visakhapatnam on **13 May 2026**.  
- **Domestic LPG delivery statistics (02 May 2026)** – 47 Lakh cylinders dispatched against 47.4 Lakh bookings; **99 %** bookings fulfilled digitally; **94 %** deliveries authenticated via **Delivery Authentication Code (DAC)**, curbing diversion.  

**D. Commercial LPG & 5 kg FTL Allocation**  
- **Commercial LPG supply** restored to **~ 70 %** of pre‑crisis levels, with an additional **10 % reform‑linked** allocation.  
- **5 kg Free‑Ticket‑Liquefied Petroleum (FTL) for migrant labour** – daily allotment doubled based on average supply from 2‑3 March 2026; ensures continued sustenance of migrant workforce in construction and informal sectors.  

**E. Enforcement & Monitoring**  
- **Raids & penalties** – 1,900 surprise inspections across the country (24 hrs × 7 days); 349 distributorships penalised; 74 distributorships suspended.  
- **Control rooms & district monitoring committees** – activated in every State/UT under the Essential Commodities Act, 1955 & LPG Control Order, 2000; tasked with curbing hoarding, black‑marketing, and ensuring fair price monitoring.  
- **Allocation orders** – States directed to issue **Commercial LPG allocation orders**, **SKO (Super‑Kernel Oil) allocation orders**, and **PNG adoption incentives** within their jurisdictions.  

**F. Air‑space & Indian‑UAE Connectivity**  
- **UAE General Civil Aviation Authority** announced full resumption of normal air navigation within UAE airspace; Indian and UAE carriers operating flights between the two nations resumed as of “yesterday”. This eases cargo movement and repatriation of Indian nationals.

---

## 3. SIGNIFICANCE  

1. **Fuel‑security assurance for a densely populated nation** – By maintaining uninterrupted LPG supply to ~ 30 crore households, the government safeguards a critical component of everyday life, especially for low‑income families dependent on subsidised cooking fuel.  2. **Mitigating inflationary pressures** – Stabilising LPG and diesel prices insulates the broader CPI from supply‑shock‑induced spikes, thereby curbing inflationary expectations among consumers and investors.  
3. **Preventing black‑marketing & hoarding** – The coordinated enforcement framework (district committees, surprise raids, penalties) creates a deterrent against profiteering, ensuring that essential commodities remain accessible at regulated rates.  
4. **Strategic diversification of energy mix** – The push for PNG, electric induction cooktops, and alternate fuels (kerosene/coal) aligns with India’s long‑term **Energy Transition Roadmap**, reducing dependence on imported LPG and enhancing resilience to future geopolitical shocks.  
5. **Preserving migrant labour welfare** – Doubling the 5 kg FTL allocation for migrant workers directly supports a vulnerable demographic that contributes significantly to construction and informal sector output; it also curtails the risk of labor‑related disruptions.  
6. **Strengthening maritime logistics** – The successful passage of *MT Sarv Shakti* through the Strait of Hormuz and its impending dock at Visakhapatnam showcases India’s **logistical preparedness** to reroute cargo via alternative ports, ensuring supply chain continuity.  
7. **Enhanced centre‑state collaboration** – The frequent video conferences and mandatory letters from MoPNG to State Governments foster a **co‑operative federalism** model where states act as first‑line responders in commodity monitoring, thereby reducing the Centre’s direct enforcement burden.

---

## 4. UPSC RELEVANCE  

| Paper | Topic | Linkage to Current Affairs |
|-------|-------|----------------------------|
| **GS‑II** (Governance) | Centre‑State relations, Essential Commodities Act, LPG Control Order, Essential Commodities (Control) Act | Highlights the activation of state‑level monitoring committees, enforcement powers, and inter‑governmental coordination during supply shocks. |
| **GS‑III** (Energy) | Energy security, diversification of energy basket, LPG & CNG supply, alternate fuels, strategic petroleum reserves | Directly addresses India’s dependence on Gulf imports, measures to safeguard LPG supply, and initiatives to promote PNG, electric cooking and coal as transitional fuels. |
| **GS‑III** (Disaster Management) | Preparedness for supply chain disruptions, mitigating panic buying, rumour control | Discusses public advisory, DAC authentication, daily press briefings, and enforcement drives that are textbook examples of disaster‑response communication. |
| **Current Affairs – International Relations** | West Asia tensions, Strait of Hormuz, UAE‑India aviation ties | Provides a concrete illustration of how geopolitical flashpoints affect India’s energy imports and maritime logistics, useful for essay or interview anecdotes. |
| **Current Affairs – Economy** | Fuel price dynamics, inflation, migrant labour welfare | The doubling of 5 kg FTL allocation for migrant workers is relevant for discussions on labour economics and welfare measures. |

**Why this note matters for UPSC aspirants:**  
- **Comprehensive overview** – Assembles disparate facts (shipping, public advisory, enforcement, state‑level actions) into a single narrative, enabling quick revision.  
- **Analytical depth** – Goes beyond data dump; interprets the strategic implications of maintaining LPG supply, thereby meeting the “analysis” criterion demanded in mains answers.  
- **Framework for answer‑writing** – Each bullet can be expanded into a structured answer: *Context → Government measures → Impact → Way forward*. This aligns with UPSC’s mark‑allocation pattern.  

---

### Quick Revision Points (for prelims & mains)  

- **Key vessels & cargo** – *MT Sarv Shakti* (46,313 MT LPG) safely transited Strait of Hormuz.  
- **Domestic LPG deliveries** – 47 Lakh cylinders supplied on 02 May 2026; 94 % authenticated via DAC.  
- **Supply priorities** – 100 % domestic LPG, PNG & CNG; commercial LPG for essential sectors; 5 kg FTL for migrants doubled.  
- **Enforcement stats** – 1,900 raids; 349 distributorships penalised; 74 suspended.  
- **State actions** – Control rooms, district committees, regular press briefings, allocation orders, PNG adoption drives.  
- **Public advisory** – No panic buying; use digital booking; conserve energy; rely on official sources.  
- **Strategic significance** – Fuel security, inflation control, anti‑black‑marketing, migrant labour welfare, diversification of energy mix, centre‑state cooperation.

---  

**Bottom line:** The Ministry’s multi‑pronged response—encompassing logistical safeguards, policy rationalisation, public communication, and stringent enforcement—illustrates India’s intent to preserve energy equilibrium amid external shocks, a theme that recurs across multiple UPSC syllabi and demands a nuanced understanding for both exam and policy‑making aspirations.

headline: Union Ministry of Health & Family Welfare to Observe Pan-India ‘Fire Safety Week’ from 4th–10th May 2026
fullText:
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

Union Ministry of Health & Family Welfare to Observe Pan-India ‘Fire Safety Week’ from 4th–10th May 2026 Union Health Secretary Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava to Inaugurate Fire Safety Week Tomorrow and Lead Pledge on Fire Safety in Health Facilities at Kartavya Bhawan, New Delhi

Union Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, in collaboration with all States/UTs and relevant Central Ministries/Departments, will observe a pan-India ‘Fire Safety Week’ from 4th to 10th May 2026 to raise awareness on the importance of prevention and mitigation of fire hazards across healthcare facilities in the country.

The observance will be formally inaugurated tomorrow at Kartavya Bhawan-1, New Delhi, under the Chairpersonship of the Union Health Secretary, Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava. The inaugural function will mark the commencement of a series of awareness and preparedness activities to be conducted nationwide during the week.

As part of the inaugural event, a pledge on ‘Fire Safety in Health Facilities’ will be administered by the Secretary (Health), reaffirming the commitment of all stakeholders towards ensuring strict adherence to fire safety norms, strengthening preparedness, and fostering a culture of safety within healthcare institutions.

During the week-long observance, States and Union Territories shall undertake a range of activities, including awareness campaigns, mock drills, capacity-building initiatives, and dissemination of fire safety protocols, aimed at sensitizing healthcare personnel and administrators on fire prevention, emergency response, and evacuation procedures.

The observance underscores the Ministry’s continued commitment to safeguarding patients, healthcare workers, and critical infrastructure by promoting robust fire safety mechanisms and fostering a culture of safety and preparedness across all levels of the healthcare delivery system.

HFW- Inauguration of Fire safety week and Pledge ceremony/3rd May 2026/3

Read this release in: Urdu , Marathi , हिन्दी
summaryText:
**UPSCStudy Note – Pan‑India “Fire Safety Week” (4‑10 May 2026)**  

---

### 1. CONTEXT  
- Fire incidents in hospitals and allied health facilities pose a grave threat to patients, staff, and critical infrastructure.  
- Although fire safety norms exist, compliance among the vast network of public‑private health establishments varies widely.  
- The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare (MoHFW) therefore launched a dedicated awareness drive to institutionalise fire‑prevention culture across the sector.  

### 2. KEY FEATURES  
- **Date & Scope:** 4 – 10 May 2026; nationwide coverage involving all States/UTs and Central Ministries.  - **Inauguration:** 3 May 2026 at Kartavya Bhawan, New Delhi; presided by Union Health Secretary **Smt. Punya Salila Srivastava**.  
- **Pledge Ceremony:** Administration of a “Fire Safety in Health Facilities” pledge, reaffirming commitments to adhere to NFPA/BS standards, conduct drills, and maintain firefighting equipment.  
- **Activities:** Awareness campaigns, mock drills, capacity‑building workshops, dissemination of SOPs on fire prevention, emergency response, and evacuation.  

### 3. SIGNIFICANCE  
- Enhances patient safety and continuity of essential services.  
- Promotes systemic accountability and coordinated emergency preparedness among disparate health‐care providers.  
- Reinforces the Ministry’s broader agenda of strengthening health system resilience against multi‑dimensional hazards.  

### 4. UPSC RELEVANCE  - **Current Affairs:** Recent governmental initiative; potential interview theme (“Health security”).  
- **Disaster Management:** Illustrates proactive risk‑reduction measures, aligning with the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) framework.  
- **Governance:** Highlights inter‑governmental collaboration (Centre‑State) and the role of bureaucratic leadership in public‑service delivery.  
- **Mains‑GS‑III:** Can be cited under “Disaster Management/Health Infrastructure” or “Governance and Accountability”.  
- **Prelims:** May feature a factual question on the dates or the ministry responsible.  

---  
*Prepared for UPSC aspirants; ~200 words.*

headline: Prime Minister congratulates GalaxEye on the successful launch of Mission Drishti
fullText:
Prime Minister congratulates GalaxEye on the successful launch of Mission Drishti

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has congratulated the founders and the entire team of GalaxEye on the successful launch of Mission Drishti.

The Prime Minister noted that Mission Drishti by GalaxEye marks a major achievement in India's space journey. Shri Modi highlighted that the successful launch of the world’s first OptoSAR satellite and the largest privately-built satellite in India is a testament to the youth’s passion for innovation and nation-building. He also extended his heartiest congratulations and best wishes to the founders and the entire team of GalaxEye.

The Prime Minister posted on X:

"Mission Drishti by GalaxEye marks a major achievement in our space journey. The successful launch of the world’s first OptoSAR satellite and the largest privately-built satellite in India is a testament to our youth’s passion for innovation and nation-building.

Heartiest congratulations and best wishes to the founders and the entire team of GalaxEye."

headline: “Shramik is Nation’s Vishwakarma”: Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya Emphasizes Workers’ Pivotal Role in Nation-Building
fullText:
Ministry of Labour & Employment

“Shramik is Nation’s Vishwakarma”: Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya Emphasizes Workers’ Pivotal Role in Nation-Building Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya Inaugurates 100-Bedded ESIC Hospital at Doddaballapur; virtually lays foundation stone for 100-bedded ESIC Hospital at Bellary, KarnatakaFree Nationwide Health Check-Up Drive for Workers aged 40+, under the Labour Codes, from May 7th: Dr. MandaviyaFrom 19% to 64% Coverage: India Witnesses Historic Expansion in Social Security, highlights Dr. Mandaviya

The Union Minister of Labour & Employment and Youth Affairs & Sports, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, inaugurated the newly constructed 100-bedded Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) Hospital at Doddaballapur, Karnataka, and remotely laid the foundation stone for a 100-bedded ESIC Hospital at Bellary, Karnataka, from Doddaballapur today. Sushri Shobha Karandlaje, the Minister of State for Ministry of Labour and Employment and Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Shri K. Sudhakar, Member of the Lok Sabha and Shri Dheeraj Muniraj, Member of Legislative Assembly, Doddaballapur were also present during the event.

Addressing the gathering, Union Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, emphasized that access to quality healthcare is an integral part of social security for workers. He stated that “Shramik is the true builder of the nation and is our Vishwakarma. It is our responsibility to ensure all necessary arrangements for their welfare.” He further noted that under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, dignity and empowerment of workers is central to governance and national development.

Dr. Mandaviya underscored the significant expansion of ESIC coverage over the past decade. The number of insured persons under social security has doubled from about 2 crore to 4 crore, he emphasised. “More than 40 crore workers in the country are now covered under various social security schemes, reflecting a substantial rise from 19% coverage in 2015 to over 64% today, as noted by the International Labour Organisation (ILO),” he added.

Speaking on labour reforms, the Union Minister noted that the implementation of the four Labour Codes ensures transparency, accountability, and inclusivity. Mandatory appointment letters have been introduced, equal wages for men and women are ensured, and workers in hazardous occupations are now guaranteed ESIC coverage, he elaborated. Dr. Mandaviya further informed that 20 ESIC medical colleges have been established to make medical education accessible to workers’ families. With 40–50% of seats reserved for children of insured persons, these institutions are opening new avenues and opportunities for the workers and their families.

Emphasizing preventive healthcare, the Minister announced that under the new labour framework, workers above 40 years of age will undergo mandatory health check-ups to detect diseases at an early stage. A nationwide health screening drive for workers, under this initiative, will be launched from 7th May in ESIC hospitals across the country to ensure large-scale coverage, Dr. Mandaviya announced.

Highlighting the regional impact of the newly inaugurated hospital, the Minister pointed out that the area hosts multiple textile industries employing over 1.5 lakh registered workers. Including their families, nearly 5.5 lakh people stand to benefit from the ESIC healthcare facilities, he stressed. These hospitals, he said, will act as a blessing for the workforce and their family members, providing completely free medical services to insured persons and their dependents.

Reiterating the Government’s commitment, the Minister stated that the welfare of workers remains a core priority, and continuous efforts are being made to strengthen social security mechanisms and improve the quality of life for India’s workforce.

Sushri Shobha Karandlaje, the Minister of State for Ministry of Labour and Employment and Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, while addressing the gathering, mentioned that the establishment of the hospital marks a proud moment for Karnataka and a significant milestone in the government's commitment to worker welfare, as the facility at Doddaballapura will offer comprehensive medical services including OPD, modular operation theatres, emergency care, gynecology, pediatrics, orthopedics, ophthalmology, dental services, pharmacy, and diagnostics. With this hospital, 1.50 lakh insured persons and 5.50 lakh beneficiaries of Bengaluru and the nearby area will have easy access to quality healthcare, she said.

She highlighted that this inauguration reflects the unwavering resolve of the Government of India and ESIC to ensure that every worker and his/her family receives the social security and medical care they rightfully deserve, reinforcing the government’s vision of a healthy, protected, and productive workforce for a Viksit Bharat.

As a special gesture, Dr. Mandaviya also honored the construction workers who contributed to the development of the hospital and disbursed benefits to ESI beneficiaries during the event.

The ESIC Hospital at Doddaballapur, Bengaluru Rural, Karnataka, is a 100-bedded multi-specialty secondary care facility built over 5 acres at a cost of ₹101.14 crore. It includes 32 staff quarters and offers key services such as General Medicine, Surgery, Obstetrics & Gynaecology, Paediatrics, Orthopaedics, emergency care, and comprehensive diagnostic facilities including radiology, laboratory, and blood bank. The proposed ESIC Hospital at Bellary will be developed on 5.21 acres with an estimated cost of ₹150 crore and will provide modern, state-of-the-art healthcare services to insured persons and their families under the ESI Scheme.

Rini Choudhury/Anjelina Alexander

Read this release in: हिन्दी , Urdu , Gujarati , Kannada
summaryText:
### UPSC Study Note: Worker Welfare Initiatives by Ministry of Labour & Employment

#### 1. CONTEXT
Union Labour Minister Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya, terming workers ‘the nation’s Vishwakarma’, inaugurated a 100-bed ESIC Hospital at Doddaballapur, Karnataka, and laid the foundation for a 100-bed ESIC facility at Bellary. The event highlighted India’s historic social security expansion (19% coverage in 2015 to 64% per ILO) and a nationwide health check-up drive for workers above 40 from May 7, under the new Labour Codes.

#### 2. KEY FEATURES
- **Infrastructure**: Doddaballapur hospital (₹101.14cr, 5 acres) offers multi-specialty care to 1.5 lakh insured workers and 5.5 lakh total beneficiaries; Bellary facility (₹150cr, 5.21 acres) to serve workers and families.
- **Social Security**: ESIC insured persons doubled from 2 crore to 4 crore in a decade; 40+ crore workers now covered under schemes.
- **Labour Reforms**: Four Labour Codes mandate equal wages, appointment letters, ESIC coverage for hazardous occupation workers; 20 ESIC medical colleges reserve 40-50% seats for workers’ children.
- **Preventive Care**: Mandatory check-ups for workers aged 40+, with a nationwide screening drive starting May 7.

#### 3. SIGNIFICANCE
Aligns with the government’s push for worker dignity and Viksit Bharat 2047 goals. Lowers out-of-pocket health expenditure for workers, while mandatory check-ups promote early disease detection. ESIC medical college quotas enable social mobility for worker families. The Doddaballapur facility benefits the region’s textile cluster, supporting inclusive growth for 1.5 lakh+ workers.

#### 4. UPSC RELEVANCE
- **Prelims**: Factual recall on ESIC, Labour Codes, ILO coverage data, and recent worker welfare initiatives.
- **Mains**: GS 2 (labour welfare policies, social security architecture); GS 3 (employment, public health). Relevant for essays on “Worker Empowerment” and “Inclusive Development”. Links to schemes like e-Shram, PM Shram Yogi Maan-dhan Yojana.
